title: Implement multi-file splitting for large decks
description: In thumbnails.py, implement the top-level generate_thumbnails() function that chunks slides into groups of at most cols × (cols + 1), calls assemble_grid() for each chunk, writes JPEG files (single file with no suffix, or -1/-2/… suffixes for multiple chunks), and returns all output file paths.
acceptance_criteria: A deck with ≤ cols × (cols + 1) slides produces a single output file with no numeric suffix
A deck exceeding that threshold produces multiple files suffixed -1, -2, …
Each chunk contains at most cols × (cols + 1) slides
All produced file paths are returned as a list
Output files are written as JPEG

handoff_summary.completed: Renamed generate_thumbnails → _render_slide_images (private helper, unchanged logic). Implemented new top-level generate_thumbnails() with chunking, grid assembly per chunk, JPEG file writing with correct suffix rules, and path list return. Syntax verified with py_compile.
handoff_summary.remaining: CLI integration (cli.py thumbnails command) is a separate bead. Tester agent should validate chunking boundary, suffix naming, and path return.
handoff_summary.risks: Any caller of the old generate_thumbnails() that expected a list of PIL Images will break — but grep confirms no callers outside thumbnails.py itself. CLI bead must use the new signature (output_prefix, temp_dir, cols).
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should validate all acceptance criteria: single-chunk/no-suffix, multi-chunk/-1/-2 suffixes, chunk size cap, return value, JPEG format.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: CLI bead (thumbnails command) must call generate_thumbnails() with the new 4-argument signature (pptx_path, output_prefix, temp_dir, cols). The old 2-argument form no longer exists.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Renamed old generate_thumbnails to _render_slide_images (private) to preserve the internal helper semantics while freeing the public name for the top-level orchestrator. Empty-slide decks produce one file containing an empty grid (assemble_grid handles this case). out_dir is derived from output_prefix.parent, allowing callers to control output directory via the prefix path.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Key cases to test: deck with exactly cols*(cols+1) slides → single file no suffix; deck with cols*(cols+1)+1 slides → two files with -1/-2; chunk content count; return type is list[Path]; files are valid JPEGs.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: out_dir defaults to Path('.') when output_prefix has no parent component (e.g. Path('thumbnails')), which writes to the current working directory. Callers should provide an absolute path if a specific output directory is needed.

conflict_risks: CLI bead (thumbnails command) must call generate_thumbnails() with the new 4-argument signature (pptx_path, output_prefix, temp_dir, cols). The old 2-argument form no longer exists.

execution_history.summary: Implemented the top-level generate_thumbnails() function in pypptx/ops/thumbnails.py. The previous generate_thumbnails() (which returned a list of PIL Images) was renamed to _render_slide_images() as a private helper. The new public generate_thumbnails(pptx_path, output_prefix, temp_dir, cols=3) chunks slides into groups of at most cols*(cols+1), calls assemble_grid() for each chunk, writes JPEG files (single no-suffix file or -1/-2/... suffixed files for multiple chunks), and returns all output file paths.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
